svg.aria-label: Your agent is the event loop. Left zone: your code — the agent flowchart (Context, Call LLM, Tool Calls) with its own loop-back edge, separated by a vertical dotted divider from the right zone: the event loop runtime. On the right, the event loop spins as two bold curved arrows with a traveling arrowhead cursor and two stops. Stop one, the call stack: the current stage steps on and runs as a frame; while it runs, feed arrows emit four faded trace events — structure, data, control, emit — into the trace queue at the center of the loop. The frame pops, and the cursor rides the bold arrow down to stop two, idle time — the dispatcher: the four events brighten and fly through the dispatcher into TRACE MEMORY, one beat behind, and every listener receives every event: onStageAdded, onCommit, onDecision, onEmit. Then the cursor laps back for the next stage. Watching never blocks the run.
svg.text: Your agent is the event loop
your code on the left — on the right the event loop spins: a stage runs on the call stack; in the idle time its trace flushes and dispatches to your listeners, one beat behind
every step emits four trace events:
the browser spins one of these every 16ms
the captured run — scrub it, query it, ask it
Watching never sits on the hot path.
It drains in the idle time — one beat behind.
grey = JavaScript's own machinery · green = footprintjs · colors = your code & its trace events
the requestIdleCallback trick, applied to agent tracing
